title: The History of the Alphabet

segments.text: The alphabet we use today travelled a long way to reach us.
It began with the Egyptians, who drew small pictures to represent sounds.
The Phoenicians simplified these into about twenty-two letters.
The Greeks borrowed it and added vowels.
The Romans adapted the Greek letters into the form we still use.
Every letter on this page has been on a journey of thousands of years.
